Response: Coppelia Robotics AG (Zurich, Switzerland); originally developed within Toshiba R&D

Metric: Software purpose (string)
Response: Versatile and scalable robot simulation framework for rapid algorithm development, factory automation simulation, fast prototyping, verification, and digital twin creation

Response: C++, C, Python, other (Lua for scripting; Java, MATLAB, Octave, Rust supported via remote API)

Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Formerly known as V-REP, rebranded 2019. CoppeliaSim Edu is free and fully functional for academic use only. CoppeliaSim Pro is commercial. CoppeliaSim Player is a free limited viewer. Coppelia Robotics founded in 2010, acquired by Blue Workforce in 2016.

Metric: Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Installation is straightforward on Linux — download and extract. No compilation needed. OpenGL library dependencies required for display. CoppeliaSim Edu requires email registration on website before download, adding an extra step compared to pip-based tools.

Response: Error dialogs are informative and guide the user toward resolution. Some edge case errors in Lua scripting produce generic messages rather than specific diagnostics.

What is the user support model? FAQ? User forum? E-mail address to direct questions? Etc. (string)
Response: Official forum (forum.coppeliarobotics.com), comprehensive User Manual included in download, tutorial series on official website, API documentation online

Metric: Overall impression? (1 .. 10)
Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Comprehensive documentation and active forum. The GUI has a learning curve for new users due to its rich feature set. Tutorial series progressively introduces concepts well. Lua scripting knowledge required for advanced usage.

Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Under active development by Coppelia Robotics AG. CoppeliaSim organization on GitHub hosts many modular repositories (plugins, dynamics, etc.). Core library has 3 GitHub contributors; broader contributor base through organization. Commercial model means most development happens internally.

Metric: Is API documented? (yes, no, n/a)
Overall impression? (1 .. 10)
Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Highly modular plugin-based architecture. Remote API supports Python, Lua, Java, MATLAB, Octave, C, C++, Rust. Supports multiple physics engines (MuJoCo, Bullet, ODE, Newton, Vortex). Integrates with ROS and ROS2.
